code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple phrase templates for common predicates
    templates = {
        "author": "{obj} is the author of {subj}",
        "isbnNumber": "{subj} has ISBN {obj}",
        "followedBy": "{subj} is followed by {obj}",
        "precededBy": "{subj} is preceded by {obj}",
        "language": "{subj} is written in {obj}",
        "mediaType": "{subj} is available as {obj}",
        "numberOfPages": "{subj} contains {obj} pages",
        "publisher": "{subj} was published by {obj}",
        "releaseDate": "{subj} was released on {obj}",
        "firstPublicationYear": "{subj} was first published in {obj}",
        "genre": "{subj} belongs to the {obj} genre",
        "literaryGenre": "{subj} belongs to the {obj} genre",
        "country": "{subj} originates from {obj}",
        "city": "{subj} is located in {obj}",
        "state": "{subj} is in the state of {obj}",
        "capital": "{subj} has the capital {obj}",
        "leader": "{subj} is led by {obj}",
        "headquarter": "{subj} is headquartered in {obj}",
        "affiliation": "{subj} is affiliated with {obj}",
        "parentCompany": "{subj} is owned by {obj}",
        "founder": "{subj} was founded by {obj}",
        "editor": "{subj} is edited by {obj}",
        "impactFactor": "{subj} has an impact factor of {obj}",
        "frequency": "{subj} is published {obj}",
        "regionServed": "{subj} serves the region {obj}",
        "largestCity": "{subj}'s largest city is {obj}",
        "location": "{subj} is located in {obj}",
        "nickname": "{subj} is nicknamed \"{obj}\"",
        "demonym": "People from {subj} are called {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "The leader of {subj} holds the title {obj}",
        # add more as needed
    }

    phrases = []
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            phrase = tmpl.format(subj=triple.subject, obj=triple.object)
        else:
            # fallback generic phrasing
            phrase = f"The {triple.predicate} of {triple.subject} is {triple.object}"
        phrases.append(phrase)

    # Join phrases into a single coherent sentence
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        return phrases[0] + "."
    else:
        # Use commas and a final conjunction for readability
        return ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: WrittenWork. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:
